sanskrit: तत्र तं बुद्धिसंयोगं लभते पौर्वदेहिकम्। यतते च ततो भूयः संसिद्धौ कुरुनन्दन।।
transliteration: tatra taṃ buddhi-saṃyogaṃ labhate paurva-dehikam | yatate ca tato bhūyaḥ saṃsiddhau kuru-nandana ||
literal_meaning: There he regains the mental linkage of the previous body; and he strives again from there toward perfection, O joy of the Kurus.
contextual_meaning: The mind that once tasted wholeness remembers; even across lives it re-orients itself toward completion like a flower turning inevitably toward the sun.
psychological_state: Post-setback resurgence: the felt sense that prior growth was not lost, only paused, and the quiet confidence that one can resume from where one left off.
trigger_scenario: Returning to therapy after years away and discovering the insights are still alive inside you, ready to be built upon.
anchor_text: Your soul remembers its purpose and keeps reaching for wholeness, life after life.
